_readme.what: Single source of truth for Eleanora Collections. collections.html (the index) and collection.html (each place-story) render from this file. Add/remove a photo by editing one line here — never touch the HTML.
_readme.how-to-add-a-collection: Append an object to `collections` with slug (url-safe), place, region, eyebrow, cover (img path), blurb (short, factual), and a photos[] array.
_readme.how-to-make-a-photo-a-talisman: Set its `talisman` from null to { "article": "journal-xxx.html", "buyUrl": null }. article = the free story it links back to (real page). buyUrl stays null until GoDaddy Payments is live, then paste the GoDaddy Pay Link and it becomes a live buy button automatically. No redesign needed.
_readme.titles: Titles in [brackets] are Penny working titles awaiting Elena's rename pass. story fields are intentionally empty — Elena authors, then they get filled via the caption pass.
_readme.price: Optional per-photo `price` (number). Absent = standard $7 postcard, bundle/stacking eligible (Any 3 $18 · 5 $28 · 10 $50). >7 (e.g. 20) = a 'signature piece' held at that flat price, EXCLUDED from the mix & match; it renders with a gold frame + a 'not included / not eligible for discount' hover note, and a per-card price + exclusion note in its detail. The frame decides, not the collection (see prints-and-token-ladder.md).
_readme.accent: Optional per-collection `accent` (the script line under the title). Absent = the default travel line 'where i went, and what i kept back'; set a string to override, or "" to hide it (e.g. The Self-Portraits, which isn't a place).
_readme.checkout: GoDaddy Payments (pay.corraoelena.com). Buy state is mocked until then: photos with a talisman but no buyUrl show 'Prints coming soon'.

collections.blurb: Old stone, sea light, and doorways worth stopping for.

photos.title: The Cove
photos.alt: Boats on the turquoise cove at Polignano a Mare
photos.whisper: Turquoise enough to make you forget you had somewhere to be.

photos.title: The Flower Door
photos.alt: A yellow doorway with climbing flowers and a small red mailbox in Bari, Italy
photos.whisper: Every door this pretty is hiding a yes behind it.

photos.title: The Two Saints
photos.alt: A faded medieval fresco of two haloed saints on a Brindisi church wall
photos.whisper: Eight hundred years on a wall, and they still haven't looked away.

photos.title: The Blue Ape
photos.alt: A blue Piaggio Ape three-wheeler parked on a Bari street
photos.whisper: Small, blue, and in no particular hurry to be anywhere.

photos.title: Coral & Iron
photos.alt: A coral-red facade with wrought-iron balconies in Bari
photos.whisper: Coral walls, iron balconies, and a whole life behind the shutters.

photos.title: Town on the Cliff
photos.alt: The whitewashed old town perched on the cliffs at Polignano a Mare
photos.whisper: A whole town that looked at the edge of the cliff and said, close enough.

photos.title: In the Shade
photos.alt: A black vintage car parked by a doorway on a Brindisi street
photos.whisper: A black car in the shade, and the good kind of nowhere to be.

photos.title: Looking Up
photos.alt: Looking straight up a narrow alley to a slot of sky in Brindisi
photos.whisper: In the old alleys, the sky comes to you in slivers.

collections.blurb: Stone walls, still water, and one island in the blue.

photos.title: Thirteen Main Street
photos.alt: A pale stone facade with coral shutters and an iron balcony
photos.whisper: Behind those coral shutters, somebody's been happy for years.

photos.title: Mermaid's Cove
photos.alt: Waves breaking over rocks below a green Adriatic headland
photos.whisper: The sea keeps throwing itself at the rocks like it forgot how this ends.

photos.title: Church Interior
photos.alt: A baroque altar with marble columns and painted frescoes
photos.whisper: Marble and gold, and a hush that was here long before you.

photos.title: Island at Sea
photos.alt: A small green island floating in the calm blue sea off Dubrovnik
photos.whisper: One green island, and all that open water daring you toward it.

photos.title: Blue Door
photos.alt: A weathered navy plank door set in an old stone wall on Sustjepanska
photos.whisper: A blue door in a stone wall, and no way to know who's still behind it.

photos.title: Stone Loggia House
photos.alt: An old arched stone loggia house with oleander in bloom
photos.whisper: Old stone arches and oleander in bloom, taking their sweet time.

photos.title: Sunset Dock
photos.alt: A weathered wooden dock leading to a small boat, the city glowing at dusk
photos.whisper: A wooden dock, a small boat, and the city going gold behind it.

photos.title: Church on the Hill
photos.alt: A stone church and clock tower rising behind a high old-town wall
photos.whisper: A clock tower that's kept the town's time longer than anyone alive.

collections.blurb: Tiled walls, painted murals, and rooftops in the haze.

photos.title: Pink & Azulejo
photos.alt: Elena in a pink tank top seated against a geometric teal-and-yellow azulejo tile wall in Lisbon

photos.title: Praça do Comércio
photos.alt: The King José equestrian statue in Praça do Comércio at sunset

photos.title: Costa do Castelo
photos.alt: A weathered green door with an 1886 transom on Costa do Castelo

photos.title: Rooftops & Bridge
photos.alt: Lisbon rooftops and the 25 de Abril bridge seen from São Jorge castle

photos.title: Have No Enemies
photos.alt: A street-art owl on patterned azulejo holding a 'Have No Enemies' banner

photos.title: Rainbow Umbrellas
photos.alt: A canopy of rainbow umbrellas strung over a Lisbon street

photos.title: Fado Vadio
photos.alt: A painted Fado Vadio mural of a singer and woman in Alfama

photos.title: Azulejo Wall
photos.alt: A teal azulejo-tiled facade with yellow shutters

photos.title: The Ripple
photos.alt: A conceptual street-art mural of a figure touching its head, set in a stone arch

photos.title: Gilded Organ
photos.alt: A gilded baroque pipe organ and carved ceiling inside a Lisbon church

photos.title: The Cobbled Hill
photos.alt: A steep cobbled Lisbon street lined with pastel buildings

photos.title: Ginja do Bairro
photos.alt: A painted Ginja do Bairro mural of a red-haired woman with a cherry liqueur

collections.blurb: Old gods in marble, monasteries in the clouds, and the sea between.

photos.title: The Green Valley
photos.alt: A lush green Pindus valley below Meteora under dramatic clouds
photos.whisper: Down there, the world forgets to ask anything of you.

photos.title: Sun on the Ionian
photos.alt: The sun low over the hills and sea at Corfu
photos.whisper: The hour the sea turns gold, and nobody's in a hurry.

photos.title: Every Record Ever
photos.alt: A woman in a black dress before a wall covered in vintage vinyl record sleeves
photos.whisper: Every record on that wall was somebody's whole summer once.

photos.title: Leaning
photos.alt: A marble woman leaning on a small column, a museum piece
photos.whisper: Two thousand years standing, and still she leans like she's tired.

photos.title: The Old Kitchen
photos.alt: A folk-museum interior with dried red corn and an old corn-sheller
photos.whisper: A kitchen that fed everyone who came through, and remembered them all.

photos.title: Goddess in Magenta
photos.alt: A headless draped marble goddess against a magenta museum wall
photos.whisper: No head, no name, and she still owns the whole room.

photos.title: The Yellow Door
photos.alt: A weathered old door in an ochre plaster wall with citrus leaves overhead
photos.whisper: The door stayed shut, and something green grew anyway.

photos.title: Swimming Weather
photos.alt: A turquoise Corfu bay ringed by green mountains, swimmers in the shallows
photos.whisper: The kind of blue that talks you into staying another week.

photos.title: The Old Town from the Water
photos.alt: The pastel Venetian waterfront of Corfu Old Town seen from the sea
photos.whisper: Those walls have watched every boat, and every goodbye.

photos.title: Two Saints
photos.alt: A faded Byzantine fresco of two haloed bishop-saints
photos.whisper: Cracked and faded, and still keeping their watch.

photos.title: Philopappos Hill
photos.alt: Philopappos Hill in Athens, pines and olives below the marble monument
photos.whisper: Climb high enough and even Athens learns to be quiet.

photos.title: The Six Maidens
photos.alt: The Porch of the Caryatids on the Erechtheion, Acropolis, golden marble against blue sky
photos.whisper: Six of them holding up a temple, and still making it look easy.

collections.blurb: Harbor light, a fox in the pines, and the fog that keeps the rest.
